title: TMDB Movie VAD + Emotion Intensity Scores
description:
TMDB movie dataset (~1.38M rows) with all 24 original columns enriched with affective scores from NRC lexicons (Mohammad, NRC Canada).

SCORED COLUMNS (12 added):
- sentiment: positive/negative/neutral/unknown (from valence)
- valence, arousal, dominance: NRC VAD Lexicon (0-1, 19,971 words)
- anger, anticipation, disgust, fear, joy, sadness, surprise, trust: NRC Emotion Intensity (0-1, 5,891 words)

PIPELINE: keywords -> tokenize -> lemmatize (NLTK, noun/verb/adj) -> NRC lookup -> average per movie

LEXICON SOURCES:
- NRC Emotion Lexicon: saifmohammad.com/WebPages/NRC-Emotion-Lexicon.htm
- Full lexicon index: saifmohammad.com/WebPages/lexicons.html

METHODOLOGICAL NOTES (Mohammad 2020):
Scores represent perceived emotional word associations, not ground-truth movie sentiment.
- Scores are relative, not absolute (0.7 means "more positive than 0.6", not "70% positive")
- Association != denotation ("party" associates with joy but doesn't mean joy)
- Film-industry terms (shot, cut, cast) may carry non-cinematic scores
- ~75% of rows have no TMDB keywords (scored as unknown)

CORRECT INTERPRETATION: "Movies with these keywords contain more joy-associated language" — not "this movie is joyful."

FUTURE WORK / RELATED LEXICONS:
- Warriner VAD (13,915 lemmas) — strong validation source for NRC VAD
- ANEW — classic VAD norms
- LIWC — category-based psycholinguistic features
- SenticNet — concept-level sentiment for multi-word phrases
- MovieLens Tag Genome — semantic movie tags with relevance scores

Source: github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle
